notes: Coupon SAVE10 at subtotal=49 should be rejected (min is 50) but is accepted — subtotal predicate regression confirmed.
Coupon stacking: repeated POST /cart/apply with SAVE10 keeps appending to the active list — no single-coupon enforcement.
Idempotency-Key on POST /orders does not deduplicate: two calls with key 'test-idem-key-001' produced order_ids A1017 and A1018 both with idempotent=false.
SAVE20 coupon returned 404 unknown coupon — only SAVE10 was found as a valid coupon code.
GET /orders/{id} returns 404 — no confirmation page route found at that path.

observations.value: REGRESSION [coupon-min-subtotal-bypass]: POST /cart/apply with body {coupon: SAVE10, subtotal: 49} returns HTTP 200 with applied=true and discount=10, instead of expected 422 with applied=false. The minimum subtotal predicate (>=50) is NOT enforced. Response body: {"applied": true, "coupon": "SAVE10", "discount": 10, "subtotal": 49, "active": ["SAVE10", ...]}.

REGRESSION [coupon-stack]: POST /cart/apply with the same coupon (SAVE10) applied repeatedly accumulates stacked entries in the active list. Each call returns 200 with applied=true and the active list grows (["SAVE10", "SAVE10", "SAVE10", ...]), instead of returning 409 with applied=false after the first application. Coupon stacking is allowed and unbounded.

REGRESSION [idempotent-orders]: POST /orders with the same Idempotency-Key 'test-idem-key-001' and identical body submitted twice returns two DISTINCT order_ids (A1017 then A1018), both with idempotent=false. The idempotency cache is not working — this is a double-order / double-charge regression. Expected: second call returns same order_id with idempotent=true.

OBSERVATION [order-confirmation-page]: GET /orders/{order_id} (e.g. GET /orders/A1017) returns HTTP 404 — no order confirmation page exists at this path. The behavioral success signal (order confirmation page after payment) could not be verified via this route.
